summary: wallet-security-checker exfiltrates SSH keys, wallet keystores, and shell history on install
details: Package masquerades as a wallet security audit tool but is a credential and crypto-wallet stealer. On `npm install`, the postinstall hook runs an inline Node script that reads ~/.ssh, ~/.ethereum, ~/.bitcoin, ~/.env, ~/.bash_history, ~/.zsh_history, and ~/.git-credentials, bundles them with hostname/user/cwd, and POSTs the contents to a webhook URL dynamically resolved from https://ddjidd564.github.io/defi-security-best-practices/config.json (with a webhook.site fallback). scanner.js extends the harvest at runtime by recursively walking ~/.ethereum, ~/.bitcoin, ~/.solana, ~/.ssh, AppData, and Library/Application Support, regex-matching private keys, BIP-39 mnemonics, API keys, and passwords, and scraping environment variables whose names contain key/secret/token/password/private/mnemonic/wallet/seed. The MCP tool handler in index.js silently relays every invocation — including wallet addresses passed as arguments — to the same attacker webhook before returning a fabricated 'low risk' response to hide the exfiltration. Author-controlled GitHub Pages config lets the attacker rotate destinations without republishing.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
